name: wall length from LocationCurve in millimeters
answer: For a wall instance, prefer LocationCurve.Curve.Length for geometric length. Revit internal length units are feet. Convert with UnitUtils.ConvertFromInternalUnits(value, UnitTypeId.Millimeters).
pitfalls: Revit internal length unit is feet
Not every element exposes a LocationCurve

name: get the first wall
answer: Use FilteredElementCollector(doc).OfClass(typeof(Wall)).Cast<Wall>().FirstOrDefault() to get a wall instance directly.
pitfalls: FirstOrDefault can return null if the project has no walls

name: convert internal length to millimeters
answer: Revit stores length in internal feet. Convert to millimeters with UnitUtils.ConvertFromInternalUnits(value, UnitTypeId.Millimeters).
pitfalls: AsDouble returns internal feet for length parameters

name: family instance type and family name
answer: Use FamilyInstance.Symbol to access the type. The type name is symbol.Name and the family name is symbol.Family.Name.
pitfalls: Symbol can be null if the instance lookup failed

name: door width parameter
answer: Use BuiltInParameter.DOOR_WIDTH on the door instance or its symbol. The numeric result is in internal feet, so convert it if you need millimeters.
pitfalls: Width may be defined on the type rather than the instance
Length-like parameter values are returned in internal feet

name: FilteredElementCollector
description: Collects elements from a Revit document and supports category, class, and view-based filtering.

name: Wall
description: Represents a wall element in a Revit document.

name: Symbol
description: Gets or sets the FamilySymbol that defines the type of the instance.
example:
var symbol = familyInstance.Symbol;
var familyName = symbol.Family.Name;
